titleStmt.title: Letter from Thomas J. O'Connell to Matthew Nathan, 10 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Thomas J. O'Connell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Thomas J. O'Connell (b.1885), secretary for the Tralee and Dingle Railway Company, to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939). O'Connell acknowledges Nathan's previous correspondence regarding an amendment to the Tralee and Dingle (committee of management), order 1896.Sir Matthew Nathan was the under secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/6375

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Tralee and Dingle Railway Company. Secretary's Office, Tralee 10th April, 1916. SIR :- With further reference to your letter (4339 - 6th inst.)
transmitting draft of an Order proposed to be made amending the
Tralee & Dingle (Committee of Management) Order 1896 I now beg to
inform you that the Tralee & Dingle Railway Company approve of same. I am, SIR,
Your obedient servant, T J O'Connell
Secretary. The Under Secretary, D U B L I N C A S T L E, D U B L I N. Resubmit on 1st May
E.O.F.
11:4:16
